The Black Bay Chrono has become one of Tudor’s standout modern releases, blending the brand’s dive watch heritage with a true chronograph layout. This version immediately catches the eye with its bright pink dial, a color that pushed the model into the spotlight the moment it was released. Limited availability and strong demand have made this configuration one of the most talked-about Black Bay Chronos on the market.

This reference features a 41mm stainless steel case with a black anodized aluminum tachymeter bezel and a pink dial with contrasting chronograph subdials and a date display at 6 o’clock. It’s powered by the Tudor caliber MT5813 automatic chronograph movement with a column wheel, silicon balance spring, and approximately 70 hours of power reserve. It comes fitted on a stainless steel Jubilee-style bracelet with a folding clasp.

CONDITION: Excellent pre-owned condition. Very few, if any, signs of wear. No dings or dents.

INCLUDED: Watch, outer box, inner box, warranty card, booklets, and White tag. 

SHIPPING: I will always ship Priority Overnight, via FedEx, to your nearest FedEx location with full insurance, at no extra cost to you! I can also meet in person in the Orange County area. More than happy to hop on a FaceTime call to show you the watch and answer any questions. 

REFERENCES AND REVIEWS: Please don't hesitate to contact me with any questions or concerns, I am always available! I have tons of positive reviews online. More than happy to show any references you need.

TRADES / SOURCING: Interested in trading in your current watch, or watches? We're always happy to make a trade-in work! Are you looking for a similar variation of this watch? Don't hesitate to reach out, we can source nearly any watch you’re looking for - even if it's not currently listed!

EXTRA PICS or VIDEOS: Please ask for any additional pictures or videos you may need! You deserve to be 100% confident in your decision and see exactly what you will be receiving. We record videos of the entire packaging process for insurance purposes, we are more than happy to send that video to you as well, just ask!

The Black Bay Chrono “Pink” might not be for everyone, and that’s OK because few of them will ever be made. The Black Bay Chrono was already quite a bold watch even before this new dial colour was introduced. Since the launch in 1970 of the Oysterdate model, TUDOR’s first chronograph, the brand has always produced watches that are closely tied to the world of motorsport. Since 1954, TUDOR has likewise been constantly improving its professional divers’ watches. The Black Bay Chrono model seamlessly fuses these traditions in a sports chronograph, with contrasting sub-counters and a high-performance automatic Manufacture Calibre, with column wheel construction and vertical clutch. The Black Bay Chrono “Pink” isn’t for the mainstream. Only a small number of examples will be produced.

This is my first Tudor and it has been an absolutely stunning watch to own. I am selling it to make room for my next Tudor.

The Black Bay Heritage has been one of my primary daily wear pieces in my collection. It has been worn at least once or twice a week and is in excellent working condition, keeping time as it should.

Cosmetically, the watch presents very well. There are minor surface scratches consistent with light, regular wear. There is a small scratch on the lower left lug, visible in the photos, which is barely noticeable unless it catches the light at the right angle. The clasp and lower portion of the bracelet show light scuff marks from desk wear. Overall, it remains in very good condition and looks fresh and well cared for.

It fits my 7-inch wrist perfectly.

Included:
• Inner and outer box
• User manual
• Warranty card
• Original tag
• Certificate of authenticity
• All original bracelet links (bracelet is currently sized for my wrist)

At the time of my purchase, I spend a few hundred dollars, and I had the watch certified. The certificate of authenticity as an enhancement to the watch’s provenance included with the watch at no cost to buyer.

Please feel free to reach out with any questions.

The Tudor Black Bay Chrono Ref. 79360N-0019 is one of the most distinctive modern releases from Tudor’s Black Bay collection, combining vintage-inspired chronograph design with a bold and contemporary aesthetic. This model features a 41mm stainless steel case paired with a striking pink dial and contrasting black sub-dials, creating a unique “reverse panda” layout that stands out while remaining highly legible. The fixed tachymeter bezel reinforces the watch’s motorsport heritage and adds to its sporty character.

Powered by the Tudor Manufacture Caliber MT5813 automatic chronograph movement, this watch delivers exceptional performance, precision, and durability. The movement is COSC-certified and offers an impressive 70-hour power reserve, making it suitable for extended wear. The dial features Tudor’s signature snowflake hands with luminous markers, along with a date display at 6 o’clock for added practicality. Completed with a stainless steel bracelet featuring the T-fit rapid adjustment clasp, this model offers both comfort and versatility for everyday wear.

Condition
This watch is in excellent pre-owned / unworn condition and comes as a complete set. It includes the original inner and outer boxes, warranty card dated 2024, booklets, and accessories. Please review the photos carefully for an accurate representation. Light handling marks, if any, are minimal and do not affect the functionality of the watch.

Technical Data

Reference: 79360N-0019
Case diameter: 41 mm
Case: Stainless steel
Bezel: Fixed tachymeter bezel with black anodized aluminum insert
Dial color: Pink with black sub-dials
Crystal: Sapphire crystal
Water resistance: 200 meters / 660 feet
Bracelet: Stainless steel bracelet with T-fit clasp
Movement: Tudor Manufacture Caliber MT5813 automatic
Power reserve: Approximately 70 hours
Functions: Hours, minutes, seconds, chronograph, date

The 79190 is commonly classified as part of the "transitional" models for the Rolex & Tudor family. Here are some common features to decipher these:

-39mm Oyster case
-Matte black dial
-CHUNKY notches on the aluminum Bezel
-Tritium lume that has aged "CREAMILY"
-Sapphire crystal

The rave for "transitional" references is rooted in the intersection between vintage design cues and modern accoutrements. For this CRISPY example dating back to 1993, the same principals apply.

The Tudor Black Bay Chrono 79360N-0024 is a bold and vibrant take on the classic Black Bay Chrono design. Crafted from stainless steel, the 41mm case combines durability with a polished, contemporary aesthetic. The standout feature is its turquoise dial, which adds a pop of modern color to the watch’s vintage-inspired design. Black subdials provide a striking contrast, maintaining excellent legibility and emphasizing the timepiece’s chronograph functionality.

Powered by the COSC-certified MT5813 movement, the 79360N-0024 offers robust performance, a 70-hour power reserve, and reliable precision. The combination of heritage design cues, a fresh dial color, and Tudor’s well-known build quality make this watch an exciting addition to the Black Bay Chrono family. Ideal for collectors and enthusiasts, it’s a striking choice that stands out from traditional chronograph designs.

What's Special about this Watch? | What makes this Watch Special?
It was the first Tudor Submariner reference to employ a unidirectional bezel on a ratchet system and the first Tudor Submariner to be fitted with sapphire crystals.
The model has a polished and brushed steel tonneau-shaped case made by Rolex, screwed down case back, "Twinlock" winding-crown, 12-hour unidirectional steel rotating bezel, sapphire crystal with cyclops lens. 
Today, vintage Tudor Submariners are far rarer than Rolex ones and they represent collectors that are often on the other end of the horological spectrum.

If you're looking for something classic with timeless distinctive design, it has everything you need from vintage submariner - “mercedes” hour’s hand, sapphire crystal with cyclops lens on date indicator.
And even more, what distinguishes from other submariners - steel rotating bezel which gives a stunning look.

Has very similar look to the Submariner 6538 that Sean Connery made famous in Dr. No.

Also it could be a perfect unisex watch. The smaller 36mm case wears comfortable, light and thin on the wrist.

Shipping to Continental US only.

Case #283 — Tudor Black Bay 58 “Navy Blue” Ref. 79030B

Status: Active Investigation
Condition: Very Good Pre-Owned

Case Notes

The Tudor Black Bay 58 “Navy Blue” Ref. 79030B is one of the strongest modern expressions in the Black Bay line — pairing vintage-inspired proportions with a crisp blue colorway that gives the watch a slightly bolder, more contemporary presence. At 39mm, it carries the balanced, highly wearable profile that has made the BB58 one of Tudor’s most broadly appreciated modern sport models.

This reference stays true to the appeal of the original Black Bay 58 formula: compact sizing, strong everyday versatility, and a design language that draws from Tudor’s historic dive watches without feeling overly nostalgic. The navy blue dial and matching blue aluminum bezel insert give it a distinct personality while keeping the watch firmly in classic dive-watch territory.

This example is a 2023 watch and is presented in very good pre-owned condition, showing only light wear consistent with normal careful use. The case, bezel, and bracelet show only faint signs of wear overall. It includes box, papers, and cards, and is currently sized to fit approximately a 7.5-inch wrist. There is one minor mark on the bezel at the 11 position, and the bracelet is short one link.

️ The Evidence

Reference: 79030B / M79030B-0001
Model: Tudor Black Bay 58 “Navy Blue”
Case: Stainless Steel
Case Size: 39 mm
Bezel: Unidirectional steel bezel with blue anodized aluminum insert
Crystal: Domed Sapphire Crystal
Dial: Blue Dial
Movement: Tudor Manufacture Calibre MT5402 (COSC) Automatic
Bracelet: Stainless Steel Bracelet
Bracelet Fit: Approximately 7.5" wrist; short 1 link
Water Resistance: 200 m
Year: 2023
Accessories: Box, Papers, and Cards

Condition Notes: Very good pre-owned condition with light signs of wear consistent with normal use. Case, bezel, and bracelet show faint wear overall. One minor bezel mark is present at the 11 position. No hang tag.

Diving Heritage

The Black Bay 58 takes its inspiration from Tudor’s late-1950s dive watch design language, especially the brand’s early 200-meter divers and the proportions collectors continue to favor today. The “58” name itself references 1958, the year Tudor introduced the reference 7924, also known as the “Big Crown.”

In blue, the model adds a modern twist to that vintage-rooted formula. It keeps the compact case, domed crystal, and classic dive-watch layout intact, while offering a colorway that feels fresh without sacrificing versatility.

Collector Provenance

Examples from recent production years that include box, papers, and cards continue to hold strong appeal, especially within the Black Bay 58 line where full-set presentation remains an important value point for many buyers. This example also benefits from the desirable bracelet configuration.

The 39mm case size, blue dial, and strong everyday wearability have helped make the BB58 one of Tudor’s most popular modern references. It remains one of the easiest contemporary sport watches to recommend to both newer buyers and seasoned collectors.

Celebrity Provenance

The Black Bay line has become one of the most recognizable modern Tudor families, appreciated by collectors, enthusiasts, and public figures alike for its balance of vintage influence and modern practicality. Its broad appeal comes from doing the fundamentals exceptionally well: size, legibility, durability, and daily versatility.

From the Case File

“Recovered as a 2023 Tudor Black Bay 58 Ref. 79030B with box, papers, and cards. Presented in very good pre-owned condition with light overall wear, one minor bezel mark at 11, and bracelet short one link.”

️ BST Verdict

Case #283 delivers one of Tudor’s most desirable modern dive watches in a highly wearable 39mm format. With its blue execution, strong full-set presentation, and only minor honest wear to note, it stands as a compelling example of the Black Bay 58 line.

The Tudor Black Bay Chrono Ref. 79360N-0019 is one of the most distinctive modern releases from Tudor’s Black Bay collection, combining vintage-inspired chronograph design with a bold and contemporary aesthetic. This model features a 41mm stainless steel case paired with a striking pink dial and contrasting black sub-dials, creating a unique “reverse panda” layout that stands out while remaining highly legible. The fixed tachymeter bezel reinforces the watch’s motorsport heritage and adds to its sporty character.

Powered by the Tudor Manufacture Caliber MT5813 automatic chronograph movement, this watch delivers exceptional performance, precision, and durability. The movement is COSC-certified and offers an impressive 70-hour power reserve, making it suitable for extended wear. The dial features Tudor’s signature snowflake hands with luminous markers, along with a date display at 6 o’clock for added practicality. Completed with a stainless steel bracelet featuring the T-fit rapid adjustment clasp, this model offers both comfort and versatility for everyday wear.

The Black Bay Chrono “Pink” might not be for everyone, and that’s OK because few of them will ever be made. The Black Bay Chrono was already quite a bold watch even before this new dial colour was introduced. Since the launch in 1970 of the Oysterdate model, TUDOR’s first chronograph, the brand has always produced watches that are closely tied to the world of motorsport. Since 1954, TUDOR has likewise been constantly improving its professional divers’ watches. The Black Bay Chrono model seamlessly fuses these traditions in a sports chronograph, with contrasting sub-counters and a high-performance automatic Manufacture Calibre, with column wheel construction and vertical clutch. The Black Bay Chrono “Pink” isn’t for the mainstream. Only a small number of examples will be produced.

Quick Facts:
The Black Bay “Monochrome” represents Tudor’s modern evolution of its classic dive watch. The fully black dial and bezel give the watch a clean, understated look while maintaining the signature snowflake hands and vintage-inspired design language that define the Black Bay line.

Inside is the in-house MT5602-U movement, certified as a METAS Master Chronometer, offering exceptional accuracy, magnetic resistance, and a robust 70-hour power reserve. Combined with 200 meters of water resistance and a solid stainless steel case, it remains a true professional-grade dive watch while still being versatile enough for everyday wear.

This example is a 2024 complete set and comes with the original box, warranty card, and accessories. A clean modern Tudor diver that combines classic design with updated technical performance.

The Black Bay 41 Monochrome, Reference 7941A1A0NU, offers a refined balance of modern design and classic styling. It features a 41mm stainless steel case with polished and satin finishes, complemented by a domed black dial with applied hour markers and a domed sapphire crystal. The 60-minute unidirectional stainless steel bezel with an aluminum insert adds both functionality and a sleek aesthetic.

Powering the watch is the Manufacture Caliber MT5602, a self-winding mechanical movement with a bidirectional rotor, certified by COSC and METAS for accuracy and reliability. Additional details include a screw-down crown with a rose motif in relief and a five-link stainless steel bracelet with a polished and satin finish, secured by a “T-fit” clasp for easy adjustment.

Case Notes

The Tudor Black Bay Chronograph “Panda” Ref. M79360N is one of the most sought-after modern Tudor releases — a piece that successfully blends the brand’s dive watch heritage with classic racing chronograph design.

What defines this reference is its crisp white dial paired with contrasting black subdials, creating the highly desirable “panda” configuration. The look is clean, legible, and unmistakably tied to vintage racing chronographs, while still feeling distinctly modern.

The fixed tachymeter bezel reinforces the watch’s motorsport roots, while the familiar Black Bay case architecture ensures durability and presence. At 41mm, the watch delivers a strong wrist profile while remaining comfortable for everyday wear.

This example dates to 2021 and is presented in excellent pre-owned condition, showing minimal signs of careful wear. The dial and sapphire crystal remain clean, and the case retains sharp lines.

The watch includes box, paperwork, and cards, and the bracelet is currently sized to fit approximately a 7.5-inch wrist.

The Black Bay Chronograph line represents Tudor’s ability to merge two core identities: diving heritage and motorsport timing. The result is a watch that feels equally at home on land or at the track.

The panda dial configuration is especially prized among collectors for its strong contrast and historical ties to vintage chronographs.

Collector Provenance

Examples that include box, paperwork, and cards, particularly from early production years like 2021, remain highly desirable among collectors.

The M79360N “Panda” has become one of the most recognizable and in-demand modern Tudor chronographs.

Celebrity Provenance

Tudor chronographs have gained increasing visibility among athletes, collectors, and enthusiasts who appreciate the brand’s blend of heritage design and modern engineering.

Tudor 1926 36mm – Silver "Waffle" Texture Dial & Rose Gold Accents – Ref. 91450

Description: For sale is the elegant Tudor 1926 (Ref. 91450-0001), a masterclass in affordable luxury. This 36mm example features the stunning Silver Opaline Embossed Dial paired with warmth-adding Rose Gold applied indices and hands. Ideally proportioned, it captures the spirit of vintage Rolex "Bubblebacks" but with modern finishing and reliability.

Why This Watch:

The Dial Texture: The embossed "waffle" pattern creates incredible depth, shifting from white to silver depending on the light. It offers a level of finishing rarely seen at this price point.

The "Goldilocks" Size: At 36mm with a slim ~9.2mm profile and 44mm lug-to-lug, this slides easily under a dress cuff but has enough presence for daily wear.

Rose Gold Warmth: The rose gold hands and numerals prevent the watch from looking "sterile" like many all-steel silver watches, adding a touch of dressy flair.

Condition Report:

Case: 36mm Polished Stainless Steel.

Bracelet: Original Tudor 7-row Jubilee-style bracelet (Satin outer links / Polished center links). Known for its exceptional comfort and articulation.

The Tudor Black Bay Chrono Ref. 79360N-0019 is one of the most distinctive modern releases from Tudor’s Black Bay collection, combining vintage-inspired chronograph design with a bold and contemporary aesthetic. This model features a 41mm stainless steel case paired with a striking pink dial and contrasting black sub-dials, creating a unique “reverse panda” layout that stands out while remaining highly legible. The fixed tachymeter bezel reinforces the watch’s motorsport heritage and adds to its sporty character.

Powered by the Tudor Manufacture Caliber MT5813 automatic chronograph movement, this watch delivers exceptional performance, precision, and durability. The movement is COSC-certified and offers an impressive 70-hour power reserve, making it suitable for extended wear. The dial features Tudor’s signature snowflake hands with luminous markers, along with a date display at 6 o’clock for added practicality. Completed with a stainless steel bracelet featuring the T-fit rapid adjustment clasp, this model offers both comfort and versatility for everyday wear.

What's Special about this Watch? | What makes this Watch Special?
It was the first Tudor Submariner reference to employ a unidirectional bezel on a ratchet system and the first Tudor Submariner to be fitted with sapphire crystals.
The model has a polished and brushed steel tonneau-shaped case made by Rolex, screwed down case back, "Twinlock" winding-crown, 12-hour unidirectional steel rotating bezel, sapphire crystal with cyclops lens. 
Today, vintage Tudor Submariners are far rarer than Rolex ones and they represent collectors that are often on the other end of the horological spectrum.

If you're looking for something classic with timeless distinctive design, it has everything you need from vintage submariner - “mercedes” hour’s hand, sapphire crystal with cyclops lens on date indicator.
And even more, what distinguishes from other submariners - steel rotating bezel which gives a stunning look.

Has very similar look to the Submariner 6538 that Sean Connery made famous in Dr. No.

Also it could be a perfect unisex watch. The smaller 36mm case wears comfortable, light and thin on the wrist.

Shipping to Continental US only.

Case #283 — Tudor Black Bay 58 “Navy Blue” Ref. 79030B

Status: Active Investigation
Condition: Very Good Pre-Owned

Case Notes

The Tudor Black Bay 58 “Navy Blue” Ref. 79030B is one of the strongest modern expressions in the Black Bay line — pairing vintage-inspired proportions with a crisp blue colorway that gives the watch a slightly bolder, more contemporary presence. At 39mm, it carries the balanced, highly wearable profile that has made the BB58 one of Tudor’s most broadly appreciated modern sport models.

This reference stays true to the appeal of the original Black Bay 58 formula: compact sizing, strong everyday versatility, and a design language that draws from Tudor’s historic dive watches without feeling overly nostalgic. The navy blue dial and matching blue aluminum bezel insert give it a distinct personality while keeping the watch firmly in classic dive-watch territory.

This example is a 2023 watch and is presented in very good pre-owned condition, showing only light wear consistent with normal careful use. The case, bezel, and bracelet show only faint signs of wear overall. It includes box, papers, and cards, and is currently sized to fit approximately a 7.5-inch wrist. There is one minor mark on the bezel at the 11 position, and the bracelet is short one link.

️ The Evidence

Reference: 79030B / M79030B-0001
Model: Tudor Black Bay 58 “Navy Blue”
Case: Stainless Steel
Case Size: 39 mm
Bezel: Unidirectional steel bezel with blue anodized aluminum insert
Crystal: Domed Sapphire Crystal

Bracelet Fit: Approximately 7.5" wrist; short 1 link

Condition Notes: Very good pre-owned condition with light signs of wear consistent with normal use. Case, bezel, and bracelet show faint wear overall. One minor bezel mark is present at the 11 position. No hang tag.

Diving Heritage

The Black Bay 58 takes its inspiration from Tudor’s late-1950s dive watch design language, especially the brand’s early 200-meter divers and the proportions collectors continue to favor today. The “58” name itself references 1958, the year Tudor introduced the reference 7924, also known as the “Big Crown.”

In blue, the model adds a modern twist to that vintage-rooted formula. It keeps the compact case, domed crystal, and classic dive-watch layout intact, while offering a colorway that feels fresh without sacrificing versatility.

Collector Provenance

Examples from recent production years that include box, papers, and cards continue to hold strong appeal, especially within the Black Bay 58 line where full-set presentation remains an important value point for many buyers. This example also benefits from the desirable bracelet configuration.

The 39mm case size, blue dial, and strong everyday wearability have helped make the BB58 one of Tudor’s most popular modern references. It remains one of the easiest contemporary sport watches to recommend to both newer buyers and seasoned collectors.

Celebrity Provenance

The Black Bay line has become one of the most recognizable modern Tudor families, appreciated by collectors, enthusiasts, and public figures alike for its balance of vintage influence and modern practicality. Its broad appeal comes from doing the fundamentals exceptionally well: size, legibility, durability, and daily versatility.

From the Case File

“Recovered as a 2023 Tudor Black Bay 58 Ref. 79030B with box, papers, and cards. Presented in very good pre-owned condition with light overall wear, one minor bezel mark at 11, and bracelet short one link.”

️ BST Verdict

Case #283 delivers one of Tudor’s most desirable modern dive watches in a highly wearable 39mm format. With its blue execution, strong full-set presentation, and only minor honest wear to note, it stands as a compelling example of the Black Bay 58 line.

Every case deserves a closer look.

Quick Facts:
The Black Bay “Monochrome” represents Tudor’s modern evolution of its classic dive watch. The fully black dial and bezel give the watch a clean, understated look while maintaining the signature snowflake hands and vintage-inspired design language that define the Black Bay line.

Inside is the in-house MT5602-U movement, certified as a METAS Master Chronometer, offering exceptional accuracy, magnetic resistance, and a robust 70-hour power reserve. Combined with 200 meters of water resistance and a solid stainless steel case, it remains a true professional-grade dive watch while still being versatile enough for everyday wear.

This example is a 2024 complete set and comes with the original box, warranty card, and accessories. A clean modern Tudor diver that combines classic design with updated technical performance.

The Tudor Black Bay Chrono Ref. 79360N-0019 is one of the most distinctive modern releases from Tudor’s Black Bay collection, combining vintage-inspired chronograph design with a bold and contemporary aesthetic. This model features a 41mm stainless steel case paired with a striking pink dial and contrasting black sub-dials, creating a unique “reverse panda” layout that stands out while remaining highly legible. The fixed tachymeter bezel reinforces the watch’s motorsport heritage and adds to its sporty character.

Powered by the Tudor Manufacture Caliber MT5813 automatic chronograph movement, this watch delivers exceptional performance, precision, and durability. The movement is COSC-certified and offers an impressive 70-hour power reserve, making it suitable for extended wear. The dial features Tudor’s signature snowflake hands with luminous markers, along with a date display at 6 o’clock for added practicality. Completed with a stainless steel bracelet featuring the T-fit rapid adjustment clasp, this model offers both comfort and versatility for everyday wear.

Condition
This watch is in excellent pre-owned / unworn condition and comes as a complete set. It includes the original inner and outer boxes, warranty card dated 2024, booklets, and accessories. Case #286 — Tudor Black Bay Chronograph “Panda”  Ref. M79360N

Status: Active Investigation
Condition: Excellent Pre-Owned
Scope: Box, Paperwork, and Cards
Year: 2021

Case Notes

The Tudor Black Bay Chronograph “Panda” Ref. M79360N is one of the most sought-after modern Tudor releases — a piece that successfully blends the brand’s dive watch heritage with classic racing chronograph design.

What defines this reference is its crisp white dial paired with contrasting black subdials, creating the highly desirable “panda” configuration. The look is clean, legible, and unmistakably tied to vintage racing chronographs, while still feeling distinctly modern.

The fixed tachymeter bezel reinforces the watch’s motorsport roots, while the familiar Black Bay case architecture ensures durability and presence. At 41mm, the watch delivers a strong wrist profile while remaining comfortable for everyday wear.

This example dates to 2021 and is presented in excellent pre-owned condition, showing minimal signs of careful wear. The dial and sapphire crystal remain clean, and the case retains sharp lines.

The watch includes box, paperwork, and cards, and the bracelet is currently sized to fit approximately a 7.5-inch wrist.

Racing Meets Heritage

The Black Bay Chronograph line represents Tudor’s ability to merge two core identities: diving heritage and motorsport timing. The result is a watch that feels equally at home on land or at the track.

The panda dial configuration is especially prized among collectors for its strong contrast and historical ties to vintage chronographs.

Collector Provenance

Examples that include box, paperwork, and cards, particularly from early production years like 2021, remain highly desirable among collectors.

The M79360N “Panda” has become one of the most recognizable and in-demand modern Tudor chronographs.

Tudor chronographs have gained increasing visibility among athletes, collectors, and enthusiasts who appreciate the brand’s blend of heritage design and modern engineering.

This excellent Tudor Black Bay 41 SMILEY Blue bezel model is available FULLY SERVICED to Perfection- under 5 seconds per day average, the Tudor Black Bay reference: 79220B-0002 (Production date is L550xxx- 2014) and as you may have been aware marked the very first of the Heritage Black Bay Revival timepieces selected by Tudor to make it's comeback in style.
The watch is available with a Black pre-owned original Tudor NATO which is a single pass strap, it also has it's original double clasp 22 mm bracelet, as this production date was 2014- it was well past it's service interval, and it was duly serviced with much aplomb and ceremony.
Tudor ETA 2824-2 Description: The Tudor modified Black Bay ETA 2824-2 is a very highly modified movement, and some of the parts inside it are only available on this 79220B (Or 79220R- 79220N) models- these include a higher main-plate, stepped center seconds wheel with totally modified thicker pinions with a step in between- modified escapement wheel- and modified Kif shock absorbers, plus screw type regulation of the seconds daily rate, and on the dial side the entire assembly is modified, cannon pinion, hour wheel, minute wheels- you are not going to find those parts in a regular ETA 2824-2, they only appear in this iteration of the Tudor Black Bay.
I did not detail the case/ only the bracelet was lightly detailed with a satinizing wheel. There are usage marks on the bezel, and it is not possible to take out this insert, because taking out the bezel- will break the Nylon pilons that secure the bezel ring to the case- in theory, the whole Nylon ring and pilons have to be replaced, this is a costly affair and Tudor in the U.S. do not yet have service stations setup to undertake this repair, even though it's sister brand Rolex have a similar system in place in almost all their newer models, and the Rolex system is available, but Tudor does not have it, so there is no possibility of having that done. I left this bezel as I received it.
The whole case was ultrasonically cleaned, and as the service came out impeccably, only proceeded with the final tests.
The crystal has a light scratch mark at 1 o'clock that is not visible to the camera, but the bezel scratches are distinct- esp. at the 6 o'clock or 30 minute mark at the bezel. The bezel works just excellently- the watch could be a superb fully serviced beater for the special person looking to ride this one out, as it has 2 bracelets, but no box/papers

For sale is a vintage circa 1989 Tudor Prince Oysterdate Mini-Sub 200m, model/reference number 73090, with the original box and papers. The papers do not list the exact purchase date, so it remains unknown. The dial is stamped “T Swiss Made T,” and the lume on the dial and hands is original. The watch is running perfectly with no issues and is keeping accurate time. It was serviced in-house by us and comes with a 1-year warranty.

Condition: Cosmetically, the watch has obvious signs of wear. There are many visible scratches and scuffs on the case, bracelet, and crystal. The crystal is missing a small chunk around the 11 o’clock area. The bracelet has noticeable stretch (sag). The lume on the dial and hands no longer glows. Lastly, due to the watch's age this watch is no longer water resistant.

Sizing: Approximately 33mm in diameter, 17mm in lug width, 11.9mm in case thickness, and 39.1mm from lug tip to lug tip.

Features: Stainless steel case, Tudor bracelet stamped "6249 03" on the clasp and "599" on the end links, acrylic crystal, screw-down crown, screw-back case, and ETA Cal. 2671 automatic movement with 25 jewels.

Shipping & Returns: Free shipping within the USA. International shipping is $100. Buyer is responsible for any taxes or import fees. Returns are accepted within 3 days if the item is returned in the same condition as sold.

This is my first Tudor and it has been an absolutely stunning watch to own. I am selling it to make room for my next Tudor.

The Black Bay Heritage has been one of my primary daily wear pieces in my collection. It has been worn at least once or twice a week and is in excellent working condition, keeping time as it should.

Cosmetically, the watch presents very well. There are minor surface scratches consistent with light, regular wear. There is a small scratch on the lower left lug, visible in the photos, which is barely noticeable unless it catches the light at the right angle. The clasp and lower portion of the bracelet show light scuff marks from desk wear. Overall, it remains in very good condition and looks fresh and well cared for.

It fits my 7-inch wrist perfectly.

Included:
• Inner and outer box
• User manual
• Warranty card

• Certificate of authenticity
• All original bracelet links (bracelet is currently sized for my wrist)

The Black Bay Chrono “Pink” might not be for everyone, and that’s OK because few of them will ever be made. The Black Bay Chrono was already quite a bold watch even before this new dial colour was introduced. Since the launch in 1970 of the Oysterdate model, TUDOR’s first chronograph, the brand has always produced watches that are closely tied to the world of motorsport. Since 1954, TUDOR has likewise been constantly improving its professional divers’ watches. The Black Bay Chrono model seamlessly fuses these traditions in a sports chronograph, with contrasting sub-counters and a high-performance automatic Manufacture Calibre, with column wheel construction and vertical clutch. The Black Bay Chrono “Pink” isn’t for the mainstream. Only a small number of examples will be produced.

Case Notes

The Tudor Black Bay Chronograph “Panda” Ref. M79360N is one of the most sought-after modern Tudor releases — a piece that successfully blends the brand’s dive watch heritage with classic racing chronograph design.

What defines this reference is its crisp white dial paired with contrasting black subdials, creating the highly desirable “panda” configuration. The look is clean, legible, and unmistakably tied to vintage racing chronographs, while still feeling distinctly modern.

The fixed tachymeter bezel reinforces the watch’s motorsport roots, while the familiar Black Bay case architecture ensures durability and presence. At 41mm, the watch delivers a strong wrist profile while remaining comfortable for everyday wear.

This example dates to 2021 and is presented in excellent pre-owned condition, showing minimal signs of careful wear. The dial and sapphire crystal remain clean, and the case retains sharp lines.

The watch includes box, paperwork, and cards, and the bracelet is currently sized to fit approximately a 7.5-inch wrist.

The panda dial configuration is especially prized among collectors for its strong contrast and historical ties to vintage chronographs.

Offered is a Tudor Black Bay Chrono reference 79360N-0002 in very good pre-owned condition, with box and papers.

The Black Bay Chrono is a masterclass in blending the brand's aquatic heritage with its long-standing history in motorsports. Nicknamed the 'Panda' for its high-contrast aesthetic, this model features a stunning opaline dial with contrasting black circular-grained sub-dials. As highlighted by Teddy Baldassarre and Monochrome, this reference successfully marries the DNA of the Black Bay line-featuring the iconic 'snowflake' hands-with the racing spirit of the 1970s. The fixed anodized aluminum bezel with its tachymetric scale completes its bold, technical character.

The 41 mm stainless steel case is built for both performance and luxury, maintaining a robust 200 meters of water resistance. The screw-down pushers provide a tactile, vintage feel while ensuring modern reliability. Presented on the signature riveted steel bracelet, it captures the essence of mid-century tool watches while providing the comfort and security of contemporary engineering. The combination of a brushed and polished finish allows this 'Panda' to transition effortlessly from the track to the dinner table.

Powering this chronograph is the Manufacture Calibre MT5813. This COSC-certified movement is a high-performance engine featuring a column-wheel and a vertical clutch, derived from the Breitling B01 movement with exclusive finishing and a silicon hairspring. With a substantial 70-hour power reserve, it stands as one of the most technically impressive movements in its class, offering both extreme precision and rugged durability.

- Brand: Tudor

The Tudor Submariner or "Snowflake" as its affectionately referred to by collectors was first seen in '68 with the ref. 7016/0 (no date), helping the brand set a new path from Rolex. The ref. 7021/0 (date) version arrived at the same time, proceeding the ref. 9401/0 (no date) and 9411/0 (date) which came in early to mid 70s. The nickname came from their distinctive squared indices and "Snowflake" like hour/seconds hands. This additional luminous material aided it in being a highly capable tool watch that we now know today has well-documented military use being issued to the French Marine Nationale and other countries' armed forces. Out of all the vintage tool watches the "Snowflake" is one of our absolute favorite choices for its distinctive design and special aura that nice examples can convey.
This particular black example, circa '71 is a more desirable "no date" variant and remains in nice vintage condition. Notably, these early "Snowflakes," especially the ref. 7016 suffered from dial bubbling so it is more difficult to find one in the state we have here which is smooth and free of distracting wear. The hands have been redone in the past and the insert is a "long 5" from an earlier watch but still genuine and pairs with the watch well - the pip was replaced in service. The case remains thick and the Tudor folded link bracelet shows light stretch and will fit an approximate 7 inch wrist. This piece has been fitted with a True Dome crystal for added protection and clarity. For anyone who wants a "Snowflake" to wear as a daily driver these pieces are some of the best vintage watches for various nato straps and this particular piece would make for a great opportunity to wear and enjoy properly in the field.

Dial: Very nicely preserved for this ref. which often suffers from bubbling. This example has cleanly aged plots and the dial has aged smoothly with no distracting wear to the naked eye.

Bezel insert: Is an earlier "long 5" so it is not correct for this watch but it is a genuine part and pairs well with the watch. The pip is a service replacement.

Hands: Are matching. They have been color matched in the past.

Case: Honest signs of use consistent with age. The lugs remain thick. Correct signed crown.

Bracelet: Is a Tudor signed folded link retaining 10 links. It displays light stretch and will fit an approximate 7 inch wrist.

Crystal: Clear. I has just been fitted with a True Dome for added clarity and protection.

Accessories: NA

The movement is running well on our timegrapher and comes with our 6-month mechanical warranty.

The Tudor Prince Date reference 74033 sits firmly in the lineage of Tudor’s long-running Datejust-adjacent models, combining Rolex-derived case architecture with Tudor’s own positioning as a more approachable, everyday counterpart. Produced in the early 1990s, this reference reflects a period when Tudor leaned heavily into versatility, offering robust automatic movements, familiar proportions, and two-tone configurations that worked equally well in professional and casual settings.

This example is notable for its IBM caseback engraving, part of a long tradition of corporate-issued watches commissioned for milestones, retirements, or executive recognition. IBM-signed watches occupy an interesting space within vintage collecting, especially when paired with Rolex and Tudor, as the company frequently partnered with top-tier Swiss manufacturers. While Rolex IBM Datejusts are the most commonly discussed, Tudor examples are far less frequently encountered, adding an extra layer of interest for collectors who appreciate provenance beyond the dial.

The champagne dial is classic for the reference, offering a warm contrast against the steel and yellow gold case and bracelet. The fluted yellow gold bezel ties the watch visually to its Rolex sibling, while the overall execution remains restrained and practical, true to Tudor’s design philosophy during this era.

What I like about this watch is how naturally the details come together without trying to steal attention. The two-tone case feels balanced, the champagne dial has just enough warmth, and the IBM engraving adds a personal, almost archival quality to the piece. It feels like a watch that lived a real life before ending up here, worn daily rather than kept away. If you appreciate corporate-signed watches but want something a bit less obvious than a Rolex Datejust, this hits a very satisfying middle ground.

Every watch is thoroughly vetted, carefully described with complete transparency, and guaranteed authentic. This watch is offered in honest condition.

Formed in 1946 by Rolex founder Hans Wilsdorf, Tudor was intended to be a brand providing more affordable watches that remained faithful to the high standards for which Rolex was renowned. Tudor Black Bay GMT Pepsi 41MM. Ref.79830RB-0002 (2018)

The stainless steel case comes with a brown leather strap. It features a bidirectional rotating bezel with a stainless steel ring and a red-and-blue (Pepsi) aluminum insert displaying 24-hour (GMT) markers.

The black dial is complemented by luminous silver Snowflake hands and luminous hour markers. A minute track runs along the outer rim. The watch has an analog time display, with luminous hands and markers, and a date window positioned at 3 o’clock.

Powered by the Tudor Calibre MT5652 automatic movement, it includes 26 jewels, operates at a frequency of 28,800 beats per hour, and offers a power reserve of approximately 70 hours.

The watch is equipped with a scratch-resistant sapphire crystal, a screw-down crown, and a solid case back. The round case measures 41mm in diameter, with a 22mm lug width. It features a folding clasp with a safety lock for secure wear.

With water resistance up to 200 meters (660 feet), this timepiece offers functionalities including GMT, dual time zones, hours, minutes, seconds, and date display.

The Tudor Black Bay 58 GMT Ref. M7939G1A0NRU is one of the newest and most exciting additions to Tudor’s highly sought-after Black Bay lineup. Released in late 2024, this model brings the beloved 39mm Black Bay Fifty-Eight case size together with a highly functional GMT complication, making it an ideal everyday travel watch.

This example is dated December 2024 and comes as a complete set with the original box and papers. With a current MSRP of $5,475, it offers an excellent opportunity to own one of Tudor’s most in-demand modern sports watches.

The watch features the classic Black Bay aesthetic with a striking black dial, luminous hour markers, and Tudor’s iconic snowflake hands for exceptional legibility. The bi-color GMT bezel allows for tracking multiple time zones, making it both practical and visually distinctive while maintaining the vintage-inspired design language that defines the Black Bay collection.

Inside beats Tudor’s in-house automatic GMT movement, designed for precision, durability, and everyday wear. The robust construction and comfortable 39mm case size make this watch incredibly versatile whether worn casually, professionally, or while traveling.

The Tudor Black Bay 58 GMT has quickly become one of the most desirable modern Tudor releases thanks to its balanced proportions, classic styling, and useful complication. Offered with box and papers and a recent 12/2024 date, this is an outstanding example ready for its next owner.

The Black Bay Chrono “Pink” might not be for everyone, and that’s OK because few of them will ever be made. The Black Bay Chrono was already quite a bold watch even before this new dial colour was introduced. Since the launch in 1970 of the Oysterdate model, TUDOR’s first chronograph, the brand has always produced watches that are closely tied to the world of motorsport. Since 1954, TUDOR has likewise been constantly improving its professional divers’ watches. The Black Bay Chrono model seamlessly fuses these traditions in a sports chronograph, with contrasting sub-counters and a high-performance automatic Manufacture Calibre, with column wheel construction and vertical clutch. The Black Bay Chrono “Pink” isn’t for the mainstream. Only a small number of examples will be produced.

Tudor Pelagos FXD Reference#M25717N-0001


The 2025 Tudor Pelagos FXD is a serious modern tool diver, built with military-grade ruggedness and practicality. Housed in a 42 mm titanium case with a satin-brushed finish, it features fixed strap bars machined into the case to increase strength under stress. With a lug-to-lug of about 52 mm and thickness circa 12.75-13 mm, it wears substantial yet balanced. Powering the watch is Tudor’s Manufacture Calibre MT5602: automatic winding, COSC-certified, non-magnetic silicon hairspring, and a “weekend-proof” ~70-hour power reserve. Water resistance is rated to 200 meters. The bezel is bidirectional, in titanium, with a ceramic insert and a 60-minute retrograde (count-down style) graduation—geared towards navigation and diving-oriented timing. The dial of the standard (non-special edition) version is navy blue, with applied luminous indices and “snowflake” hands for excellent legibility. It comes equipped with a navy blue single-piece fabric strap with grey central threading, plus a complimentary rubber strap.

The Tudor Ranger is the brand’s modern interpretation of its mid-century field watches, drawing clear lineage from the Oyster Prince Rangers worn on exploration expeditions in the 1950s. This current reference ref. M79930-0001 brings that utilitarian spirit into a contemporary context with modern manufacturing standards, a robust in-house movement, and a clean, highly legible dial. The matte black dial, Arabic numerals, and broad hands are deliberately straightforward, prioritizing clarity and function over excess design. It is a watch rooted in Tudor’s tool-watch identity and intended to be worn daily without pretense.

This is one of the most honest watches Tudor makes. The proportions are well judged, the bracelet is solid and comfortable, and the dial layout feels timeless rather than trendy. For someone who wants a no-nonsense daily watch with real heritage behind it, the Ranger makes a compelling case.

Every watch is thoroughly vetted, carefully described with complete transparency, and guaranteed authentic. This watch is offered in honest condition.

*Shipping*
We exclusively use FedEx Express for shipping. We prefer shipping to a FedEx Office and Ship Center for security reasons, that being said we will consider shipping directly to your residence or business on a case-by-case basis

*International Shipping*
We can consider shipping to select international locations. Please note that we will not under-declare the product on the commercial invoice used for customs. All duty fees are to be paid by the buyer

*Payments*
We accept payments made via Chrono24’s Trusted Checkout Process. Per US regulations, all sales on Chrono24 are subject to the local sales tax rate of the shipping address

*Trade In*
We can consider trade-ins on a case-by-case basis. If you would like to trade watches please send us a description of your watch as well as an expected valuation

*Warranty*
On top of the manufacturer warranty, we offer a 1-year warranty on the movement. This does not include any loss or damage caused by physical damage, theft, or negligence. Should the watch require servicing we will cover it if the manufacturer’s warranty is no longer valid.

*In Person Viewings*
We do not conduct in-person transactions or viewings

*Authenticity Guarantee*
All of our products have been checked and are guaranteed authentic and in working condition

The Tudor Black Bay Chrono Ref. 79360N-0019 is one of the most distinctive modern releases from Tudor’s Black Bay collection, combining vintage-inspired chronograph design with a bold and contemporary aesthetic. This model features a 41mm stainless steel case paired with a striking pink dial and contrasting black sub-dials, creating a unique “reverse panda” layout that stands out while remaining highly legible. The fixed tachymeter bezel reinforces the watch’s motorsport heritage and adds to its sporty character.

Powered by the Tudor Manufacture Caliber MT5813 automatic chronograph movement, this watch delivers exceptional performance, precision, and durability. The movement is COSC-certified and offers an impressive 70-hour power reserve, making it suitable for extended wear. The dial features Tudor’s signature snowflake hands with luminous markers, along with a date display at 6 o’clock for added practicality. Completed with a stainless steel bracelet featuring the T-fit rapid adjustment clasp, this model offers both comfort and versatility for everyday wear.

The Black Bay Chrono is a masterclass in blending the brand's aquatic heritage with its long-standing history in motorsports. Nicknamed the 'Panda' for its high-contrast aesthetic, this model features a stunning opaline dial with contrasting black circular-grained sub-dials. As highlighted by Teddy Baldassarre and Monochrome, this reference successfully marries the DNA of the Black Bay line-featuring the iconic 'snowflake' hands-with the racing spirit of the 1970s. The fixed anodized aluminum bezel with its tachymetric scale completes its bold, technical character.

The 41 mm stainless steel case is built for both performance and luxury, maintaining a robust 200 meters of water resistance. The screw-down pushers provide a tactile, vintage feel while ensuring modern reliability. Presented on the signature riveted steel bracelet, it captures the essence of mid-century tool watches while providing the comfort and security of contemporary engineering. The combination of a brushed and polished finish allows this 'Panda' to transition effortlessly from the track to the dinner table.

Powering this chronograph is the Manufacture Calibre MT5813. This COSC-certified movement is a high-performance engine featuring a column-wheel and a vertical clutch, derived from the Breitling B01 movement with exclusive finishing and a silicon hairspring. With a substantial 70-hour power reserve, it stands as one of the most technically impressive movements in its class, offering both extreme precision and rugged durability.

The Black Bay Chrono 25 celebrates 25 years of the Alinghi Red Bull Racing team with an unprecedented proposal within Tudor: a forged carbon composite case that brings the nautical spirit into the realm of extreme performance. The contrast between the dial’s bright white and the blue accents evokes the sails of the Swiss team, while the overall design preserves the robust and functional identity of the Black Bay chronograph.

Beneath the surface, the in-house MT5813 caliber offers COSC certification, a column wheel, and a vertical clutch, consolidating Tudor’s technical maturity. Its matte carbon tachymeter bezel, hybrid fabric strap, and black pushers emphasize the sporty character of an edition that redefines the collection’s design language. The result is a high-performance timepiece with contemporary design and a true competitive spirit.

For sale is my trusty Tudor Black Bay Pro. I've had it for about three years and worn it maybe 40-50 times total. I was the second owner and purchased it in 2022 from a well-known American pre-owned dealer, just a couple of months after it was originally purchased new. For a while it had been my go-to for trips, weekends, and whenever I needed a tough, reliable watch. It’s never let me down, but it’s seen less and less wrist time over the years and deserves to be used by someone rather than sit in my watch box.

*In-house MT5652 movement with GMT and date functions.
*70 hour power reserve that can last the entire weekend even if not used or wound.
*Durable build with useful 200m water resistance. It can handle pretty much anything anyone would throw at it and is a reliable everyday workhorse.
*Signs of normal wear on the case and bracelet/clasp, but nothing out of the ordinary and nothing on the dial or crystal that would impact the function or utility of the watch.
*Sale includes the original box and original papers, including warranty card, tag, and a couple of extra bracelet links (watch has been resized to fit my 7 inch wrist.

Buyer would be responsible for shipping.

Feel free to reach out if you're interested or want more pics/info.

**Shipping**

We exclusively use FedEx Express for shipping. We prefer shipping to a FedEx Office and Ship Center for security reasons, that being said we will consider shipping directly to your residence or business on a case-by-case basis

**International Shipping**

We can consider shipping to select international locations. Please note that we will not under-declare the product on the commercial invoice used for customs. All duty fees are to be paid by the buyer

**Payments**

We accept payments made via Chrono24’s Trusted Checkout Process. Per US regulations, all sales on Chrono24 are subject to the local sales tax rate of the shipping address

**Trade In**

We can consider trade-ins on a case-by-case basis. If you would like to trade watches please send us a description of your watch as well as an expected valuation

On top of the manufacturer warranty, we offer a 1-year warranty on the movement.  This does not include any loss or damage caused by physical damage, theft, or negligence. Should the watch require servicing we will cover it if the manufacturer’s warranty is no longer valid.

**In Person Viewings**

We do not conduct in-person transactions or viewings

**Authenticity Guarantee**

All of our products have been checked and are guaranteed authentic and in working condition

The TUDOR Black Bay 41 mm is a testament to the brand's rich heritage, combining timeless design with advanced watchmaking technology. Its 41 mm stainless steel case, with a polished and satin finish, offers a bold yet sophisticated presence on the wrist. The black domed dial, protected by a domed sapphire crystal, exudes classic elegance and ensures clear readability, while the 60-minute unidirectional stainless steel bezel with an aluminum insert adds functionality and durability. Powered by the COSC-certified Manufacture Calibre MT5602-U, this self-winding mechanical movement boasts an impressive 70-hour power reserve, making it a reliable companion for any adventure. Waterproof to 200 meters, the watch is equipped with a screw-down stainless steel crown featuring the iconic TUDOR rose. Completing the design is a five-link stainless steel bracelet with a polished and satin finish, secured by the TUDOR “T-fit” clasp for easy adjustments. This unworn, like-new timepiece comes with its original box, papers, and a transferable five-year warranty dated 2024, making it an excellent choice for collectors and enthusiasts.

Formed in 1946 by Rolex founder Hans Wilsdorf, Tudor was intended to be a brand providing more affordable watches that remained faithful to the high standards for which Rolex was renowned. Vintage July 2001 timepiece perfect for either dress or field. Tudor’s reputation as a “working man’s” watch comes through in the assertive design but the delicate size (34 mm), mirrored bezel, and alternating polished and brushed portions of the jubilee link bracelet mean this watch wears equally well with a pair of jeans or a tuxedo. The dial is more indigo than blue; an unusual, deep color not found on many modern pieces. Screw down crown, Rolex cyclops date aperture, polished Arabic numerals and indices. This model features a 41mm stainless steel case paired with a striking pink dial and contrasting black sub-dials, creating a unique “reverse panda” layout that stands out while remaining highly legible. The fixed tachymeter bezel reinforces the watch’s motorsport heritage and adds to its sporty character.

Powered by the Tudor Manufacture Caliber MT5813 automatic chronograph movement, this watch delivers exceptional performance, precision, and durability. The movement is COSC-certified and offers an impressive 70-hour power reserve, making it suitable for extended wear. The dial features Tudor’s signature snowflake hands with luminous markers, along with a date display at 6 o’clock for added practicality. Completed with a stainless steel bracelet featuring the T-fit rapid adjustment clasp, this model offers both comfort and versatility for everyday wear.

NAMED ONE OF WATCHPRO MAGAZINE’S “HOT 100” - THE TOP 100 INFLUENCERS OF THE U.S. WATCH INDUSTRY. HOW CAN WE SERVE YOU?

1967 TUDOR (Rolex) PRINCE OYSTERDATE Vintage Mens Ref. 7966 Watch - Solid 18K Gold

Rolex Case, Crown & Bracelet - Original Condition - 1 Year Warranty

More and more collectors are becoming acquainted with watches from Rolex's Tudor brand, creations which have become increasingly more collectible. Watches in the Tudor line, apart from the fact that they utilize ebauche movements, are similar to those of Rolex in design and construction.

When Rolex launched the Tudor brand in 1945, they strongly intended that the public perception of its quality had to equal that of Rolex. To achieve this lofty aim, until the mid-1990's, Tudor watches featured Rolex cases and crowns, and, until 1971, Rolex bracelets.

An advertisement in Britain's Horological Journal in vintage times stated that Tudor's were, "Manufactured under strict Rolex supervision and bear the Rolex label of guarantee."

Today's offering showcases this Certified Pre-Owned Tudor Prince Oysterdate ref. 7966 in uncommon 18K gold.

In the vintage era, 18K gold Tudors occupy a uniquely uncommon position, particularly when the model in question is not a formal dress watch but a robust, Oyster-cased sports reference such as the Tudor Prince Oysterdate.

Tudor was conceived as Rolex’s tool-watch counterpart—engineered for durability, value, and everyday wear—so the overwhelming majority of Prince Oysterdate production was executed in steel or steel-and-gold. Solid 18K cases ran counter to the brand’s practical ethos and were produced only in very small numbers, often for specific markets, like certain parts of Europe and Asia or special retailer driven commissions. Tudor did not actively promote solid-gold sports Oysters the way Rolex did with Day-Dates or gold Submariners decades later.

Detailed Appraisal:

Country of origin: Switzerland

Maker: Tudor

Model: Prince Oysterdate, Ref. 7966

Serial no.: 568,108

Year built: c. 1967 - late 1966 to early 1967

Functions: Hours, Minutes, Automatic, Sweep Seconds, Semi-Quick Setting Date

Designed for: Men

Case material: 18K gold

Case markings (outside): Original Oyster Case by Rolex Geneva - Rolex coronet logo

Case condition: Its original Rolex Oyster case presents in excellent condition and is scratch-free.

Dimensions: 34mm diameter excluding crown by 41.5mm long lug tip to tip

Dial: Brushed silver, signed Tudor Oysterdate, applied very cool 18K gold vintage styled baton indexes with textured finish on the center of the primary hours, small rose logo, luminous hour dots, outer minute/seconds divisions, original and untouched, presents close to mint condition to the naked eye - just very slight aging visible under magnification

Hands: Original, gold batons, luminous. The original luminous in its hands beautifully matches the pips.

Movement type: Original, Cal. 2462, self-winding, rhodium-plated, decorated rotor, 25 jewels, straight-line lever escapement, monometallic balance, self-compensating flat balance spring

Jewels: 25 rubies

Band: Rolex Oyster bracelet, original, gold-plated, previously sized to fit a wrist up to approx. 6.75 inches, adjustable, gold wearthrough on the high points and remnants of wear We also carry vintage styled Horween type leather straps and a variety of other straps in various prints, skins, styles, and colors. Please ask us if you're interested.

Crystal: Acrylic, excellent condition

Servicing: One of our Master Watchmakers, who used to be the watchmaker for an authorized Rolex dealer and was given special training by Rolex at their Beverly Hills Service Center to be an authorized Rolex watchmaker, has fully serviced the watch which consists of fully disassembling the movement, carefully inspecting each part, replacing any overly worn components with genuine parts, installing a correct new mainspring and crystal, replacing the gaskets, reassembling and lubricating the movement, and extensive timing testing and regulation (an $895 value).

Timing: Timed to precision on a state-of-the-art computerized system. It is running very strong and true, keeping time like a new Tudor or Rolex in our testing recording position.

Warranty: 1-year international warranty.

Your watch is important to us, and we will take care of it for its lifetime. We provide the best after sale care in the business with an extremely experienced team of four Master Watchmakers all of whom each have over thirty-five years of experience and a large number of parts. The Tudor Black Bay 58 “Inter Milan” reference 79030N is a finely executed limited edition released in 2024 to celebrate Inter Milan’s second star, limited to just 1,908 numbered pieces. It retains the beloved vintage-inspired 39 mm stainless steel case with polished and satin finishes, matched with a slender 11.9 mm thickness that echoes the original 1958 divers’ proportions. The watch is powered by Tudor’s in‑house Manufacture Calibre MT5402, a COSC‑certified automatic movement with a silicon hairspring, offering approximately 70 hours of power reserve and robust precision. The standout ombré “Blu Inter” dial transitions from blue at the center to black at the periphery, accented with gilt markers and hands, and features the Inter Milan logo with two gilt stars at 6 o’clock. The unidirectional bezel in steel with a matte black anodized aluminum insert and gilt markings reinforces the team’s signature colors. Offered on a rivet‑style stainless steel bracelet or a black jacquard fabric strap with an Inter blue stripe, this watch combines sporty elegance with football heritage. Its limited‑edition status, technical pedigree, and strong collector appeal make it a compelling piece for aficionados of both horology and football culture. Like New with Tudor box, additional Tudor strap, and papers dated 2024.

Tudor introduced the Date + Day in 1969 as a more accessible counterpart to the Rolex Day-Date—affectionately nicknamed the “Poor Man’s President.” While Rolex reserved the Day-Date for precious metals (save for the exceedingly rare reference 6611) and smaller case sizes, Tudor took a different and, in our view, more exciting path. The 7019/4 is crafted in stainless steel and sized at a larger 38mm, malong this watch wear with a distinct sporty, more versatile character.

Tudor fitted these watches with an original Rolex crown, Oyster screw-down caseback, and the deditable Rolex 7836 bracelet—a charming reminder of the two brands’ heritage. Make no mistake: there’s nothing “poor” about this watch.

Our example features a striking blue sunburst dial and presented in excellent vintage condition. Powering it is the beautifully preserved A. Schild caliber 1895, and it comes complete with all original parts—an honest, character-rich piece that effortlessly bridges elegance and everyday wear.

The Tudor Black Bay Chrono Ref. 79360N-0019 is one of the most distinctive modern releases from Tudor’s Black Bay collection, combining vintage-inspired chronograph design with a bold and contemporary aesthetic. This model features a 41mm stainless steel case paired with a striking pink dial and contrasting black sub-dials, creating a unique “reverse panda” layout that stands out while remaining highly legible. The fixed tachymeter bezel reinforces the watch’s motorsport heritage and adds to its sporty character.

Powered by the Tudor Manufacture Caliber MT5813 automatic chronograph movement, this watch delivers exceptional performance, precision, and durability. The movement is COSC-certified and offers an impressive 70-hour power reserve, making it suitable for extended wear. The dial features Tudor’s signature snowflake hands with luminous markers, along with a date display at 6 o’clock for added practicality. Completed with a stainless steel bracelet featuring the T-fit rapid adjustment clasp, this model offers both comfort and versatility for everyday wear.

The watch is in very good condition showing minimal signs of use and is in excellent mechanical condition having just been serviced by our master watchmaker.

The Tudor Submariner is a unique piece in Tudor's history as it uses a Rolex case and a Tudor movement and dial making this watch a direct link between Tudor and its parent company Rolex and the iconic Rolex Submariner. M79010SG-0001 M79010SG-0002 The Tudor Black Bay Fifty-Eight 925 79010SG is encased in 39mm of 925 sterling silver surrounding a taupe dial on a brown leather strap. Features of this Tudor Black Bay Fifty-Eight include hours minutes and seconds. This Tudor watch also measures 12.9mm in thickness and 47.8mm from lug-to-lug.The use of 925 silver is a first for a modern Tudor dive watch but it is also extremely rare in the current watchmaking production. If there's nothing innovative here in the sense of something unprecedented experimental technically new it is a rather bold move by Tudor to reintroduce this forgotten metal in a watch.Introduced in 2018 this model is a smaller and even more vintage-oriented take on the BB concept that was released in 2012 a watch instrumental in the resurrection of the brand. Calibre MT5400 the same engine as all other Black Bay Fifty-Eight watch. This automatic movement relies on modern solutions such as a traversing balance bridge a variable inertia balance an anti-magnetic balance spring or a long power reserve of 70 hours. Its precision is certified by the COSC but Tudor insists on regulating its watches at -2 and +4 seconds' variation per day when assembled. The Tudor Black Bay Chrono Ref. 79360N-0019 is one of the most distinctive modern releases from Tudor’s Black Bay collection, combining vintage-inspired chronograph design with a bold and contemporary aesthetic. This model features a 41mm stainless steel case paired with a striking pink dial and contrasting black sub-dials, creating a unique “reverse panda” layout that stands out while remaining highly legible. The fixed tachymeter bezel reinforces the watch’s motorsport heritage and adds to its sporty character.

Powered by the Tudor Manufacture Caliber MT5813 automatic chronograph movement, this watch delivers exceptional performance, precision, and durability. The movement is COSC-certified and offers an impressive 70-hour power reserve, making it suitable for extended wear. The dial features Tudor’s signature snowflake hands with luminous markers, along with a date display at 6 o’clock for added practicality. Completed with a stainless steel bracelet featuring the T-fit rapid adjustment clasp, this model offers both comfort and versatility for everyday wear.

The Black Bay Chrono “Pink” is a rare, disruptive masterstroke from Tudor that challenges the traditional boundaries of sports watch aesthetics. Originally conceived through the lens of Tudor’s daring ambassadors—from the pink jerseys of the Giro d’Italia to the vibrant identity of David Beckham’s Inter Miami CF—this reference features a domed pink dial that is as technically impressive as it is visually arresting. It is a timepiece for the collector who values exclusivity and the "daring" spirit that has come to define Tudor’s modern identity.


Contained within the 41mm satin-brushed and polished steel case is the Manufacture Calibre MT5813, a high-performance COSC-certified movement featuring a column-wheel construction and vertical clutch. This "Pink Panda" configuration provides a robust 70-hour power reserve and is paired with a sophisticated 5-link stainless steel bracelet equipped with the "T-fit" rapid adjustment clasp for a tailored ergonomic fit. With its 200-meter water resistance and signature Snowflake hands, this limited-release chronograph remains a professional-grade instrument that commands a singular presence in any environment.

M79010SG-0001 M79010SG-0002 The Tudor Black Bay Fifty-Eight 925 79010SG is encased in 39mm of 925 sterling silver surrounding a taupe dial on a brown leather strap. Features of this Tudor Black Bay Fifty-Eight include hours minutes and seconds. This Tudor watch also measures 12.9mm in thickness and 47.8mm from lug-to-lug.The use of 925 silver is a first for a modern Tudor dive watch but it is also extremely rare in the current watchmaking production. If there's nothing innovative here in the sense of something unprecedented experimental technically new it is a rather bold move by Tudor to reintroduce this forgotten metal in a watch.Introduced in 2018 this model is a smaller and even more vintage-oriented take on the BB concept that was released in 2012 a watch instrumental in the resurrection of the brand. Calibre MT5400 the same engine as all other Black Bay Fifty-Eight watch. This automatic movement relies on modern solutions such as a traversing balance bridge a variable inertia balance an anti-magnetic balance spring or a long power reserve of 70 hours. Its precision is certified by the COSC but Tudor insists on regulating its watches at -2 and +4 seconds' variation per day when assembled. Tudor – Black Bay 68 – Silver Dial – 7943A1A0NU – 2025

The Tudor Black Bay 68 Silver Dial (Ref. 7943A1A0NU) is a bold evolution of Tudor’s iconic dive watch heritage—refined for the modern collector who values both presence and performance. Introduced in 2025, this model expands the Black Bay line with a larger 43 mm case, offering a commanding wrist presence while maintaining the brand’s unmistakable vintage-inspired DNA.

The standout silver dial delivers a clean yet striking aesthetic, enhanced by contrasting black accents and Tudor’s signature snowflake hands. The result is a highly legible, versatile timepiece that seamlessly transitions from everyday wear to more elevated settings. Paired with a robust stainless steel case and bracelet, this watch embodies durability without compromising elegance.

Powered by a COSC-certified automatic movement, the Black Bay 68 ensures exceptional precision and reliability. Built for both land and sea, it features impressive water resistance and a unidirectional rotating bezel—staying true to Tudor’s professional dive watch roots.

As one of the newest additions to the Black Bay family, the 7943A1A0NU represents a fresh opportunity for collectors seeking modern releases with long-term relevance. Its combination of updated proportions, clean dial execution, and Tudor’s growing market demand positions it as a strong entry point into luxury watch investment.

Tudor Oysterdate Chronograph “Big Block” – Ref. 94300 – Circa 1979–1980

Offered for sale is a Tudor Oysterdate Chronograph reference 94300, commonly referred to by collectors as the “Big Block.” Produced in the late 1970s and early 1980s, this model represents Tudor’s first automatic chronograph and remains one of the most collectible vintage Tudor references.

This example features the highly desirable silver “panda” dial with contrasting black subdials, paired with the classic stainless steel case and tachymeter bezel.

The dial shows attractive vintage patina on the hour markers and hands, consistent with the watch’s age, giving the piece strong vintage character.

The watch is pre-owned and in vintage condition, showing signs of wear consistent with age. The tachymeter bezel displays visible wear and markings, and the case and bracelet show normal surface scratches from regular use over time.

The watch is fitted on a Tudor stainless steel bracelet with Rolex signed clasp, which shows normal wear consistent with age.

The watch was recently tested on a timegrapher (January 2026) and is currently running at approximately +1 second per day with healthy amplitude, indicating strong mechanical performance.

Service history is unknown.

This is an excellent opportunity to acquire a classic vintage Tudor “Big Block” chronograph, a model that continues to grow in collector interest due to its connection to early automatic chronograph development and Rolex/Tudor heritage.

Nicknamed the 'Reverse Panda', this reference features a deep matte black dial contrasted by silver-toned, circular-grained sub-dials. As noted by Teddy Baldassarre and Monochrome, this high-contrast layout improves legibility while paying homage to the classic racing chronographs of the 1960s and 70s. The fixed black anodized aluminum bezel with a tachymetric scale underscores its technical, performance-oriented character.

The 41 mm stainless steel case is a masterclass in versatility, retaining the signature 'snowflake' hands and large winding crown of the Black Bay line. With 200 meters of water resistance, it remains a capable diver's tool despite its chronograph complications. The watch is presented on the distinctive riveted steel bracelet, offering a vintage-inspired aesthetic paired with the secure, solid feel of modern manufacturing. The screw-down pushers ensure that the chronograph remains watertight while providing a tactile, mechanical engagement.

Beneath the dial beats the Manufacture Calibre MT5813, a high-performance engine born from a collaboration with Breitling. This COSC-certified movement features a column-wheel and vertical clutch, ensuring smooth operation and extreme precision. With a 70-hour power reserve and a non-magnetic silicon hairspring, the 'Reverse Panda' is as technically proficient as it is visually striking, making it one of the most respected modern sports chronographs in horology.
